Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Paper states 'GenBank accession number OQ201607' and 'It is named as UGT79B74 by UGT Nomenclature Committee'.

Source_Species.reason: GuApiGT was identified from Glycyrrhiza uralensis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Paper explicitly uses the Latin name Glycyrrhiza uralensis.

Applied_Species.reason: GuApiGT was tested in vitro from G. uralensis cDNA and introduced into Nicotiana benthamiana for transient expression.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Glycyrrhiza uralensis and Nicotiana benthamiana are explicitly mentioned.

Applied_Promoters.reason: For tobacco experiments, GuApiGT was expressed using pEAQ-HT-DEST1 with a 35S promoter.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Results identify GuApiGT as an apiosyltransferase catalyzing flavonoid glycoside 2''-O-apiosylation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: GuApiGT is a UGT, its crystal structure shows a GT-B fold, and the paper emphasizes a unique 45-aa PSPG box with RLGSDH motif.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Candidate gene showed expression pattern similar to GuCHS/GuCHR and roots had higher apioside content; Fig. 1e shows higher expression in roots than cortex/leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: GuApiGT was transiently expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Tobacco experiments used Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression and some assays involved exogenous UDP-GlcA and isoliquiritin or isoliquiritigenin feeding.

Primary_Substrate.reason: GuApiGT converted liquiritin and isoliquiritin and accepted many glycosides; UDP-Api was the strict sugar donor.

Primary_Product.reason: Products included liquiritin apioside, isoliquiritin apioside, and many 2''-O-apiosides.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Paper states GuApiGT catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of sugar moieties on flavonoid glycosides; products were identified as β-O-glycosides in introduction/background, though β stereochemistry for each product is inferred rather than directly re-shown in Results.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: GuApiGT is presented as the missing phenolic apiosyltransferase in flavonoid apioside biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results place it in the phenolic/flavonoid glycoside apiosylation branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: GuApiGT forms apiosides from pre-existing glycosides, making it the terminal apiosylation step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: GuApiGT directly forms the target apiosides from glycoside acceptors.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper frames GuApiGT as the previously missing apiosylation step and key terminal determinant for flavonoid apioside formation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Liquiritin apioside, isoliquiritin apioside, and other flavonoid apiosides are explicit products/end metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These terminal products are flavonoid apiosides.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper states liquiritin apioside has potent antitussive activity and flavonoid apiosides are believed to play hormone-like roles.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: (Iso)liquiritin apiosides accumulated more in roots of G. uralensis and were produced in engineered tobacco leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: GuApiGT efficiently/regioselectively formed 2''-O-apiosides and enabled de novo production of at least 10 flavonoid apiosides in tobacco.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported: co-expression, cloning, enzyme assays, LC/MS, kinetics, substrate scope, crystal structure, mutagenesis, simulations, transient expression, metabolite profiling.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: UAXS was functionally coupled with GuApiGT in assays and pathway reconstruction, and GuCHS/GuCHR were co-expression bait genes, but no direct physical protein-protein interactions were demonstrated.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper reports GuApiGT has broad acceptor promiscuity but strict donor specificity; GuGT14 caused byproduct formation in pathway reconstruction; no growth penalty in tobacco is reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary accurately reflects the main Results on identification, activity, structural basis, and tobacco pathway reconstruction.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PDB 8HZZ is explicit; residues R368, S371, D372, H373, I136, and catalytic H18/D115 are all discussed in Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Paper demonstrates GuApiGT as a useful engineering enzyme for heterologous flavonoid apioside production and donor-specificity engineering.

Source_Species.reason: PtApiGT was cloned from Pueraria thomsonii.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is one of the candidate UGTs discovered using the unique 45-aa PSPG box; Fig. 7 table shows PtApiGT motif ending RLGSDY.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Function was identified by enzyme catalysis reactions in vitro.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Paper confirms PtApiGT is an apiosyltransferase by enzyme assays, but the exact wording 'flavonoid glycosides; UDP-apiose' is inferable from the assay context rather than explicitly restated for PtApiGT alone.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: As an identified apiosyltransferase in the same experimental framework, PtApiGT transfers apiose to acceptors to form apiosides.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PtApiGT is discussed in the context of discovering more apiosyltransferases for flavonoid apioside biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It belongs to the flavonoid glycoside apiosylation branch by functional characterization as an ApiGT.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Apiosyltransferases act at the terminal apiosylation step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: As an apiosyltransferase, it directly forms apioside products.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Terminal products are flavonoid apiosides in this context.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Paper states PtApiGT was identified as an apiosyltransferase from Leguminosae.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Text says they cloned PtApiGT and identified function by enzyme catalysis reactions; LC/MS is referenced in Fig. 7c/Supplementary Fig. 142.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Paper states 'GuGT53 (UGT88E28, GenBank accession number OQ266890)'.

Source_Species.reason: GuGT53 was discovered from Glycyrrhiza uralensis.

Applied_Species.reason: GuGT53 was from G. uralensis and used in engineered Nicotiana benthamiana pathway reconstruction.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Glycyrrhiza uralensis and Nicotiana benthamiana are explicit.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The tobacco reconstruction section uses pEAQ-HT-DEST1 constructs and elsewhere explicitly mentions 35S for GuApiGT, but the promoter is not restated specifically for GuGT53.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper states GuGT53 regio-selectively and efficiently catalyzed 4′/4-O-glycosylation of liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT88E28 designation supports Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase classification.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Paper states GuGT53 showed a similar expression pattern as GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: GuGT53 was transiently expressed in infiltrated tobacco leaves as part of module 3.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Used in Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in tobacco.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin are explicit substrates, but 'likely UDP-glucose donor' is inferred rather than explicitly stated in the excerpt.

Primary_Product.reason: Paper explicitly states GuGT53 converts liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin into liquiritin and isoliquiritin, respectively.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Regioselective 4′/4-O-glycosylation to form glucoside acceptors is explicitly described.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: GuGT53 functions in liquiritin/isoliquiritin formation and therefore in upstream supply for flavonoid apioside biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Paper uses GuGT53 as the upstream glycosylation step supplying acceptors for GuApiGT.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It acts upstream of GuApiGT, not terminally.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It forms immediate precursors liquiritin and isoliquiritin for downstream apiosylation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Not established is consistent with the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: GuGT53 is connected to final products liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside through precursor formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper notes bioactivity of licorice flavonoid apiosides generally, but this exact field is not directly tested for GuGT53.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Effect of GuGT53 was demonstrated in engineered tobacco leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Replacing GuGT14 with GuGT53 improved isoliquiritin apioside yield by avoiding regioselectivity-related byproduct formation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Paper reports co-expression pattern, cloning, enzyme characterization, transient pathway reconstruction, and LC/MS metabolite analysis.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: GuApiGT/GuCHS/GuCHR are functionally related or co-expressed, but direct physical interactions were not shown.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper contrasts GuGT53 with GuGT14, which caused byproduct 7a due to poor regio-selectivity; no specific tradeoff for GuGT53 was reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Summary accurately reflects discovery, catalytic role, and improved tobacco production when replacing GuGT14.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper explicitly states GuGT53 was used in module 3 to supply the correct glycosylated acceptors for GuApiGT.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Results directly support GuGT53 as a useful companion enzyme for engineering production of liquiritin/isoliquiritin apiosides.

Source_Species.reason: Paper states the UDP-apiose/UDP-xylose synthase used was from Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: UAXS was used in vitro and co-expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nicotiana benthamiana is explicit, but 'In vitro system' is not a species and the inclusion of 'NA' makes the field imprecise.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The tobacco reconstruction used pEAQ-HT-DEST1 vectors, but the text explicitly mentions 35S promoter only for GuApiGT, not UAXS specifically.

Enzyme_Name.reason: UAXS is explicitly described as UDP-apiose/UDP-xylose synthase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Its function as a UDP-sugar interconversion enzyme is directly described.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: UAXS was transiently co-expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: In assays UAXS was used with NAD+ and UDP-GlcA, and in tobacco it was co-expressed to supply UDP-apiose.

Primary_Product.reason: Paper states UAXS produces UDP-apiose and UDP-xylose.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This exact conversion is described in Introduction and Methods.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: UAXS supplies UDP-apiose for flavonoid apiosylation and is part of UDP-apiose biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It functions in UDP-sugar precursor supply.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: UAXS acts upstream of the apiosyltransferase reaction.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It supplies the precursor donor pool rather than directly making apiosides.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: UAXS is important in the engineered system, but the paper does not explicitly designate it as rate-limiting or a key control step.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Its function supports biosynthesis of flavonoid apiosides as final products.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Those final products are flavonoid apiosides.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: General functions of flavonoid apiosides are discussed, but not as a direct tested property of UAXS.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Flavonoid apiosides were produced in engineered tobacco leaves with UAXS co-expression.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: UAXS enabled GuApiGT activity in vitro and tobacco by supplying UDP-apiose; without donor supply, apiosylation would not proceed.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Coupled enzyme assays and transient co-expression in tobacco with LC/MS detection are reported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: UAXS was functionally coupled with GuApiGT, but direct protein-protein interaction was not shown.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper explicitly notes UAXS could also produce UDP-Xyl, but GuApiGT did not utilize UDP-Xyl.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Summary accurately reflects why UAXS was used and its necessity in assays and tobacco reconstruction.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Paper explicitly states UDP-Api is commercially unavailable, making UAXS experimentally essential.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Results support UAXS as an essential donor-supply module for heterologous apioside engineering.
